architecture.summary: GETER is a structure-aware generative framework that augments LLMs for explainable temporal reasoning. It ingests temporal knowledge graphs and encodes their structural information via a temporal encoder. A structure-text prefix adapter projects graph features into the text embedding space as soft tokens. These graph tokens are prepended to instruction-tuned prompts, allowing the LLM to generate explanations that integrate both structural and textual context.

biblio.title: Towards Explainable Temporal Reasoning in Large Language Models: A Structure-Aware Generative Framework

claims.statement: LLMs struggle to deliver convincing explanations when relying solely on textual information for temporal reasoning.
GETER achieves state-of-the-art performance in explainable temporal reasoning while demonstrating strong generalization capabilities.
The framework integrates temporal knowledge graphs with text to enable LLMs to generate explanations by seamlessly integrating soft graph tokens with instruction-tuning prompt tokens.
